prompt: Documentary photography style, desaturated and still. A long empty street in a bombed residential neighbourhood, viewed from ground level at dusk. The street stretches into the distance. On both sides, the facades of apartment buildings are intact on the far left but progressively more destroyed moving right — windows blown out, walls collapsed, rubble spilling onto the pavement. On the ground, running the entire length of the street, are hundreds of pairs of shoes — children's shoes, sandals, boots, worn slippers — arranged in loose rows as if their owners simply vanished mid-step. The shoes grow denser and more numerous toward the right side of the frame where the destruction is worst. No people visible anywhere. One distant streetlamp is still lit, casting a pale amber pool of light on the shoes beneath it. The rest of the image is in cold blue-grey shadow. Mood: unbearable absence, quiet accumulation of loss, the weight of the uncounted. Color palette: cold desaturated grey-blue throughout, single point of warm amber from the lamp, muted ochre of the shoes. Rule of thirds composition — the vanishing point of the street sits on the right third. Vast and oppressive sky above. Photorealistic, cinematic, no people, no movement. Absolutely no text, no words, no letters, no numbers, no labels, no legends, no chart elements, no graphs, no axes anywhere in the image.
dataset_name: ACLED Armed Conflict Location and Event Data
insight: Since 2016, over 330,000 civilians have been killed in armed conflict worldwide — most of them in wars the world has largely stopped watching.
